mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack reduces corporate wage pressures and signals economic deceleration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60d returns with a Rank IC > 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the industrial production index signals strengthening economic activity and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5d returns in >55% of cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index above 0.3% signals accelerating inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL MoM increases above 0.3% are followed by positive 20d returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation shocks that weaken economic fundamentals, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into positive corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth periods show negative 5d forward returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production expands while retail sales contract, inventory buildup signals an impending demand shock that triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Inventory buildup periods fail to predict negative 20d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).
